description: The comb: 'whila' (hair) finished with the '-ru' that ends 'kiru' (chisel) and 'katiru' (knife). The tool family grows to three, and this is its gentlest member: teeth that untangle instead of cutting.
sound_symbolism: 'whila' flows in first, all breath and liquid, and 'ru' marks it as a tool. The softest word in the tool family, correctly.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia roe whilaru whila temoe.' (I tend my hair with the comb.) Related tools: 'kiru' (chisel), 'katiru' (knife).

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The ornamental comb was one of the movement's signature objects: horn and enamel carved into dragonflies and vines, a daily tool made worth looking at.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Carved from wood, bone, or horn, and often the most personal object a person owned. Grooming was daily care made visible.
